parameters.label: Printer technology
parameters.description: Printer technology

parameters.label: Bed exclude area
parameters.description: Unprintable area in XY plane. For example, X1 Series printers use the front left corner to cut filament during filament change. The area is expressed as polygon by points in following format: \

parameters.label: Elephant foot compensation
parameters.description: Shrink the initial layer on build plate to compensate for elephant foot effect

parameters.label: Layer height
parameters.description: Slicing height for each layer. Smaller layer height means more accurate and more printing time

parameters.label: Printable height
parameters.description: Maximum printable height which is limited by mechanism of printer

parameters.label: Extruder printable height
parameters.description: Maximum printable height of this extruder which is limited by mechanism of printer

parameters.label: Hostname, IP or URL
parameters.description: Slic3r can upload G-code files to a printer host. This field should contain the hostname, IP address or URL of the printer host instance. Print host behind HAProxy with basic auth enabled can be accessed by putting the user name and password into the URL in the following format: https://username:password@your-octopi-address/

parameters.label: Device UI
parameters.description: Specify the URL of your device user interface if it's not same as print_host

parameters.label: API Key / Password
parameters.description: Slic3r can upload G-code files to a printer host. This field should contain the API Key or the password required for authentication.

parameters.label: Printer
parameters.description: Name of the printer

parameters.label: HTTPS CA File
parameters.description: Custom CA certificate file can be specified for HTTPS OctoPrint connections, in crt/pem format. If left blank, the default OS CA certificate repository is used.

parameters.label: Ignore HTTPS certificate revocation checks
parameters.description: Ignore HTTPS certificate revocation checks in case of missing or offline distribution points. One may want to enable this option for self signed certificates if connection fails.

parameters.label: Printer preset names
parameters.description: Names of presets related to the physical printer

parameters.label: Avoid crossing wall
parameters.description: Detour and avoid traveling across wall which may cause blob on surface (when travel length greater than Travel distance threshold)

parameters.label: Smoothing wall speed along Z(experimental)
parameters.description: Smoothing outwall speed in z direction to get better surface quality. Print time will increases. This does not work on spiral vase mode.

parameters.label: Avoid crossing wall - Max detour length
parameters.description: Maximum detour distance for avoiding crossing wall. Don't detour if the detour distance is larger than this value. Detour length could be specified either as an absolute value or as percentage (for example 50%) of a direct travel path. Zero to disable

parameters.label: Avoid crossing wall - Includes support
parameters.description: Including support while avoiding crossing wall.

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Cool Plate

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Cool Plate

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Engineering Plate

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the High Temp Plate

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Bed temperature for layers except the initial one. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Textured PEI Plate

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Bambu Cool Plate SuperTack

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Cool Plate

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Engineering Plate

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the High Temp Plate

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Bed temperature of the initial layer. Value 0 means the filament does not support to print on the Textured PEI Plate

parameters.label: Bed type
parameters.description: Bed types supported by the printer

parameters.label: The number of other layers print sequence
Before layer change G-code
parameters.description: This G-code is inserted at every layer change before lifting z

parameters.label: Bottom shell layers
parameters.description: This is the number of solid layers of bottom shell, including the bottom surface layer. When the thickness calculated by this value is thinner than bottom shell thickness, the bottom shell layers will be increased

parameters.label: Bottom shell thickness
parameters.description: The number of bottom solid layers is increased when slicing if the thickness calculated by bottom shells layers is thinner than this value. This can avoid having too thin shell when layer height is small. 0 means that this setting is disabled and thickness of bottom shell is absolutely determained by bottom shell layers

parameters.label: Force cooling for overhang and bridge
parameters.description: Enable this option to optimize part cooling fan speed for overhang and bridge to get better cooling

parameters.label: Fan speed for overhang
parameters.description: Force part cooling fan to be at this speed when printing bridge or overhang wall which has large overhang degree. Forcing cooling for overhang and bridge can get better quality for these part

parameters.label: Ironing fan speed
parameters.description: This part cooling fan speed is applied when ironing. Setting this parameter to a lower than regular speed reduces possible nozzle clogging due to the low volumetric flow rate, making the interface smoother. Set to -1 to disable it.

parameters.label: Pre start fan time
parameters.description: Force fan start early(0-5 second) when encountering overhangs. This is because the fan needs time to physically increase its speed.

parameters.label: Cooling overhang threshold
parameters.description: Force cooling fan to be specific speed when overhang degree of printed part exceeds this value. Expressed as percentage which indicides how much width of the line without support from lower layer. 0% means forcing cooling for all outer wall no matter how much overhang degree

parameters.label: Overhang threshold for participating cooling
parameters.description: Decide which overhang part join the cooling function to slow down the speed. Expressed as percentage which indicides how much width of the line without support from lower layer. 100% means forcing cooling for all outer wall no matter how much overhang degree

parameters.label: Bridge direction
parameters.description: Bridging angle override. If left to zero, the bridging angle will be calculated automatically. Otherwise the provided angle will be used for external bridges. Use 180°for zero angle.

parameters.label: Bridge flow
parameters.description: Decrease this value slightly(for example 0.9) to reduce the amount of material for bridge, to improve sag

parameters.label: Bridge counterbore holes
parameters.description: This option creates bridges for counterbore holes, allowing them to be printed without support. Available modes include: 1. None: No bridge is created 2. Partially Bridged: Only a part of the unsupported area will be bridged 3. Sacrificial Layer: A full sacrificial bridge layer is created

parameters.label: Top surface flow ratio
parameters.description: This factor affects the amount of material for top solid infill. You can decrease it slightly to have smooth surface finish

parameters.label: Initial layer flow ratio
parameters.description: This factor affects the amount of material for the initial layer

parameters.label: Only one wall on top surfaces
parameters.description: Use only one wall on flat top surface, to give more space to the top infill pattern. Could be applied on topmost surface or all top surface.

parameters.label: Top area threshold
parameters.description: The min width of top areas in percentage of perimeter line width.

parameters.label: Only one wall on first layer
parameters.description: Use only one wall on the first layer of model

parameters.label: Slow down for overhang
parameters.description: Enable this option to slow printing down for different overhang degree
Speed for line of wall which has degree of overhang between 10% and 25% line width. 0 means using original wall speed
Speed for line of wall which has degree of overhang between 25% and 50% line width. 0 means using original wall speed
Speed for line of wall which has degree of overhang between 50% and 75% line width. 0 means using original wall speed
Speed for line of wall which has degree of overhang between 75% and 100% line width. 0 means using original wall speed
Speed of 100%% overhang wall which has 0 overlap with the lower layer.

parameters.label: Slow down by height
parameters.description: Enable this option to slow printing down by height

parameters.label: Starting height
parameters.description: The height starts to slow down

parameters.label: Ending height
parameters.description: The height finishes slowing down, Ending height should be larger than Starting height, or the slowing down will not work!

parameters.label: Bridge
parameters.description: Speed of bridge and completely overhang wall

parameters.label: Brim width
parameters.description: Distance from model to the outermost brim line

parameters.label: Brim type
parameters.description: This controls the generation of the brim at outer and/or inner side of models. Auto means the brim width is analysed and calculated automatically.

parameters.label: Brim-object gap
parameters.description: A gap between innermost brim line and object can make brim be removed more easily

parameters.label: Compatible machine condition
parameters.description: A boolean expression using the configuration values of an active printer profile. If this expression evaluates to true, this profile is considered compatible with the active printer profile.

parameters.label: Compatible process profiles condition
parameters.description: A boolean expression using the configuration values of an active print profile. If this expression evaluates to true, this profile is considered compatible with the active print profile.

parameters.label: Print sequence
parameters.description: Print sequence, layer by layer or object by object

parameters.label: Slow printing down for better layer cooling
parameters.description: Enable this option to slow printing speed down to make the final layer time not shorter than the layer time threshold in \ , so that layer can be cooled for a longer time. This can improve the cooling quality for needle and small details

parameters.label: Don't slow down outer walls
parameters.description: If enabled, this setting will ensure external perimeters are not slowed down to meet the minimum layer time. This is particularly helpful in the below scenarios: 1. To avoid changes in shine when printing glossy filaments 2. To avoid changes in external wall speed which may create slight wall artifacts that appear like Z banding 3. To avoid printing at speeds which cause VFAs (fine artifacts) on the external walls

parameters.label: Cooling slowdown logic
parameters.description: Determines how the printer slows down when minimum layer time isn't reached. 'Uniform cooling' slows down all print features equally (current default behavior). 'Consistent surface' prioritizes slowing infill and internal perimeters first, preserving external perimeter speed for better surface finish on glossy filaments. This helps reduce VFA (Vertical Fine Artifacts) and maintains consistent surface shine.

parameters.label: Perimeter transition distance
parameters.description: Distance in millimeters before the end of slowed perimeters where the original print speed is gradually restored. This reduces quality issues when transitioning from slowed features to fast external perimeter printing. Only applies when 'Consistent surface' cooling logic is selected. Recommended value: 5-10mm. Set to 0 to disable.

parameters.label: Normal printing
parameters.description: The default acceleration of both normal printing and travel except initial layer

parameters.label: Travel
parameters.description: The acceleration of travel except initial layer

parameters.label: Short travel
parameters.description: Acceleration used for short travel moves near external perimeters. Short travels are moves shorter than the 'Retraction minimum travel' distance. Lower values (e.g., 250-500 mm/s²) reduce ringing artifacts on sharp corners without significantly impacting print time. Set to 0 to disable (uses normal travel acceleration).

parameters.label: Initial layer travel
parameters.description: The acceleration of travel of initial layer

parameters.label: Default filament profile
parameters.description: Default filament profile when switch to this machine profile

parameters.label: Default process profile
parameters.description: Default process profile when switch to this machine profile

parameters.label: Activate air filtration
parameters.description: Activate for better air filtration

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Speed of exhaust fan during printing.This speed will overwrite the speed in filament custom gcode

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Speed of exhuast fan after printing completes

parameters.label: For the first
parameters.description: Set special auxiliary cooling fan for the first certain layers.

parameters.label: Full fan speed at layer
parameters.description: Auxiliary fan speed will be ramped up linearly from layer \ to maximum at layer \ . \ will be ignored if lower than \ , in which case the fan will be running at maximum allowed speed at layer \ + 1.

parameters.label: For the first
parameters.description: Set special cooling fan for the first certain layers.The part cooling fan of the first layer used to be closed to get better build plate adhesion and used for auto cooling function

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Part cooling fan speed for the first few layers. Set to 0 to disable the part cooling fan on the initial layers for better bed adhesion

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Special auxiliary cooling fan speed, effective only for the first x layers

parameters.label: Don't support bridges
parameters.description: Don't support the whole bridge area which makes support very large. Bridge usually can be printing directly without support if not very long

parameters.label: Thick bridges
parameters.description: If enabled, bridges are more reliable, can bridge longer distances, but may look worse. If disabled, bridges look better but are reliable just for shorter bridged distances.

parameters.label: Max bridge length
parameters.description: Max length of bridges that don't need support. Set it to 0 if you want all bridges to be supported, and set it to a very large value if you don't want any bridges to be supported.

parameters.label: End G-code
parameters.description: End G-code when finish the whole printing
parameters.default: M104 S0 ; turn off temperature G28 X0  ; home X axis M84     ; disable motors

parameters.label: Between Object Gcode
parameters.description: Insert Gcode between objects. This parameter will only come into effect when you print your models object by object

parameters.label: End G-code
parameters.description: End G-code when finish the printing of this filament

parameters.label: Ensure vertical shell thickness
parameters.description: Add solid infill near sloping surfaces to guarantee the vertical shell thickness (top+bottom solid layers)

parameters.label: Vertical shell speed
parameters.description: Speed for vertical shells with overhang regions. If expressed as percentage (for example: 80%) it will be calculated on the internal solid infill speed above

parameters.label: Detect floating vertical shells
parameters.description: Detect overhang paths in vertical shells and slow them by bridge speed.

parameters.label: Internal bridge support thickness
parameters.description: When sparse infill density is low, the internal solid infill or internal bridge may have no archor at the end of line. This causes falling and bad quality when printing internal solid infill. When enable this feature, loop paths will be added to the sparse fill of the lower layers for specific thickness, so that better archor can be provided for internal bridge. 0 means disable this feature

parameters.label: Top surface pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern of top surface infill

parameters.label: Top surface density
parameters.description: Density of top surface infill, 100% means a fully solid filled top layer. Lower values create a textured top surface, at 0%, only the walls are created on the top layer. Intended for aesthetic or functional purposes, not to fix issues such as over-extrusion.

parameters.label: Monotonic line travel extend
parameters.description: Enable this option to extend the travel distance between lines, improving the adhesion between the monotonic line infill and the walls.(percent to line width)

parameters.label: Bottom surface pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern of bottom surface infill, not bridge infill

parameters.label: Bottom surface density
parameters.description: Density of bottom surface infill, 100% means a fully solid filled top layer. Lower values create a textured bottom surface, Intended for aesthetic or functional purposes, not to fix issues such as over-extrusion. WARNING: Lowering this value may negatively affect bed adhesion.

parameters.label: Internal solid infill pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern of internal solid infill. if the detect narrow internal solid infill be enabled, the concentric pattern will be used for the small area.

parameters.label: Outer wall
parameters.description: Line width of outer wall

parameters.label: Outer wall
parameters.description: Speed of outer wall which is outermost and visible. It's used to be slower than inner wall speed to get better quality.

parameters.label: Small perimeters
parameters.description: This setting will affect the speed of perimeters having radius <= small perimeter threshold (usually holes). If expressed as percentage (for example: 80%) it will be calculated on the outer wall speed setting above. Set to zero for auto.

parameters.label: Small perimeter threshold
parameters.description: This sets the threshold for small perimeter length. Default threshold is 0mm

parameters.label: Order of walls
parameters.description: Print sequence of inner wall and outer wall.

parameters.label: Print infill first
parameters.description: Order of wall/infill. false means print wall first.

parameters.label: Extruder
parameters.description: The extruder to use (unless more specific extruder settings are specified). This value overrides perimeter and infill extruders, but not the support extruders.

parameters.label: Height to rod
parameters.description: Distance of the nozzle tip to the lower rod. Used for collision avoidance in by-object printing.

parameters.label: Height to lid
parameters.description: Distance of the nozzle tip to the lid. Used for collision avoidance in by-object printing.

parameters.label: Distance to rod
parameters.description: Horizontal distance of the nozzle tip to the rod's farther edge. Used for collision avoidance in by-object printing.

parameters.label: Nozzle height
parameters.description: The height of nozzle tip.

parameters.label: Max Radius
parameters.description: Max clearance radius around extruder. Used for collision avoidance in by-object printing.

parameters.label: Extruder Color
parameters.description: Only used as a visual help on UI

parameters.label: Extruder offset
parameters.description: If your firmware doesn't handle the extruder displacement you need the G-code to take it into account. This option lets you specify the displacement of each extruder with respect to the first one. It expects positive coordinates (they will be subtracted from the XY coordinate).

parameters.label: Flow ratio
parameters.description: The material may have volumetric change after switching between molten state and crystalline state. This setting changes all extrusion flow of this filament in gcode proportionally. Recommended value range is between 0.95 and 1.05. Maybe you can tune this value to get nice flat surface when there has slight overflow or underflow

parameters.label: Object flow ratio
parameters.description: The flow ratio set by object, the meaning is the same as flow ratio.

parameters.label: Enable pressure advance
parameters.description: Enable pressure advance, auto calibration result will be overwriten once enabled. Useless for Bambu Printer

parameters.label: Pressure advance
parameters.description: Pressure advance(Klipper) AKA Linear advance factor(Marlin). Useless for Bambu Printer

parameters.label: Filament notes
parameters.description: You can put your notes regarding the filament here.

parameters.label: Process notes
parameters.description: You can put your notes regarding the process here.

parameters.label: Printer notes
parameters.description: You can put your notes regarding the printer here.

parameters.label: Default
parameters.description: Default line width if some line width is set to be zero

parameters.label: Keep fan always on
parameters.description: If enable this setting, part cooling fan will never be stopped and will run at least at minimum speed to reduce the frequency of starting and stopping

parameters.label: Layer time
parameters.description: Part cooling fan will be enabled for layers of which estimated time is shorter than this value. Fan speed is interpolated between the minimum and maximum fan speeds according to layer printing time

parameters.label: Default color
parameters.description: Default filament color

parameters.label: Color
parameters.description: Only used as a visual help on UI

parameters.label: Required nozzle HRC
parameters.description: Minimum HRC of nozzle required to print the filament. Zero means no checking of nozzle's HRC.

parameters.label: Filament map to extruder
parameters.description: Filament map to extruder

parameters.label: Filament map plus for multi nozzle
parameters.description: Filament map to the index identified by extruder and nozzle_volume_type

parameters.label: Map the logical extruder to physical extruder
parameters.description: Map the logical extruder to physical extruder

parameters.label: filament mapping mode
parameters.description: filament mapping mode used as plate param

parameters.label: Flush temperature
parameters.description: temperature when flushing filament. 0 indicates the upper bound of the recommended nozzle temperature range

parameters.label: Flush temperature
parameters.description: Flush temperature used in fast purge mode.

parameters.label: Flush volumetric speed
parameters.description: Volumetric speed when flushing filament. 0 indicates the max volumetric speed

parameters.label: Max volumetric speed
parameters.description: This setting stands for how much volume of filament can be melted and extruded per second. Printing speed is limited by max volumetric speed, in case of too high and unreasonable speed setting. Can't be zero

parameters.label: Extruder change
parameters.description: The maximum volumetric speed for ramming before extruder change, where -1 means using the maximum volumetric speed.

parameters.label: Hotend change
parameters.description: The maximum volumetric speed for ramming before a hotend change, where -1 means using the maximum volumetric speed.

parameters.label: Minimal purge on wipe tower
parameters.description: After a tool change, the exact position of the newly loaded filament inside the nozzle may not be known, and the filament pressure is likely not yet stable. Before purging the print head into an infill or a sacrificial object, Slic3r will always prime this amount of material into the wipe tower to produce successive infill or sacrificial object extrusions reliably.

parameters.label: Filament load time
parameters.description: Time to load new filament when switch filament. For statistics only

parameters.label: Filament unload time
parameters.description: Time to unload old filament when switch filament. For statistics only

parameters.label: Extruder switch time
parameters.description: Time to switch extruder. For statistics only

parameters.label: Hotend change time
parameters.description: Time to change hotend.

parameters.label: Bed temperature type
parameters.description: This option determines how the bed temperature is set during slicing: based on the temperature of the first filament or the highest temperature of the printed filaments.

parameters.label: Diameter
parameters.description: Filament diameter is used to calculate extrusion in gcode, so it's important and should be accurate

parameters.label: Adaptive volumetric speed
parameters.description: When enabled, the extrusion flow is limited by the smaller of the fitted value (calculated from line width and layer height) and the user-defined maximum flow. When disabled, only the user-defined maximum flow is applied.

parameters.label: Shrinkage
parameters.description: Enter the shrinkage percentage that the filament will get after cooling (94% if you measure 94mm instead of 100mm). The part will be scaled in xy to compensate. Only the filament used for the perimeter is taken into account. Be sure to allow enough space between objects, as this compensation is done after the checks.

parameters.label: Velocity Adaptation Factor
parameters.description: This parameter reflects the speed at which a material transitions from one state to another. It, along with the smooth coefficient, determines the final length of the transition zone. A larger value: requires a shorter transition zone. A smaller value: requires a longer transition zone to avoid flow instability.

parameters.label: Adhesiveness Category
parameters.description: Filament category

parameters.label: Metal stickiness
parameters.description: Indicates how strongly the filament tends to stick to the metal nozzle and leave residue. \ means untested or custom filament, behaves the same as Low. Low: e.g. PLA - retraction can be safely skipped in infill areas. Medium: moderate stickiness - use with caution. High: e.g. PETG - retraction should not be skipped to avoid oozing artifacts on outer walls.

parameters.label: Density
parameters.description: Filament density. For statistics only

parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: The material type of filament

parameters.label: Soluble material
parameters.description: Soluble material is commonly used to print support and support interface

parameters.label: Scarf seam type
parameters.description: Set scarf seam type for this filament. This setting could minimize seam visibiliy.

parameters.label: Scarf start height
parameters.description: This amount can be specified in millimeters or as a percentage of the current layer height.

parameters.label: Scarf slope gap
parameters.description: In order to reduce the visiblity of the seam in closed loop, the inner wall and outer wall are shortened by a specified amount.

parameters.label: Scarf length
parameters.description: Length of the scarf. Setting this parameter to zero effectively disables the scarf.

parameters.label: Extruder change
parameters.description: When changing the extruder, it is recommended to extrude a certain length of filament from the original extruder. This helps minimize nozzle oozing.

parameters.label: Hotend change
parameters.description: When changing the hotend, it is recommended to extrude a certain length of filament from the original nozzle. This helps minimize nozzle oozing.

parameters.label: Support material
parameters.description: Support material is commonly used to print support and support interface

parameters.label: Is mixed filament
parameters.description: Whether this filament slot is a mixed filament composed of multiple physical filaments

parameters.label: Mixed filament components
parameters.description: Comma-separated 1-based indices of component filaments, e.g. \

parameters.label: Mixed filament sublayer ratios
parameters.description: Comma-separated ratio values summing to 1.0, e.g. \

parameters.label: Mixed filament gradient
parameters.description: Enable Z-direction gradient mode for mixed filament sub-layers. When enabled, the sub-layer ratios vary linearly across layers.

parameters.label: Mixed filament gradient range
parameters.description: Start and end ratios for the first component in gradient mode. Comma-separated pair, e.g. \ means 10% to 90%.

parameters.label: Mixed filament gradient curve
parameters.description: Optional Photoshop-style custom curve mapping Z progress to the first component ratio. Encoded as pipe-separated control points, either \ (legacy) or \ when a tangent override is needed (empty token or \ means use PCHIP default).

parameters.label: Mixed filament per-part gradient
parameters.description: When gradient mode is enabled, apply the gradient to each part of an assembly independently rather than treating the whole assembly as one Z range.

parameters.label: Filament printable
parameters.description: The filament is printable in extruder

parameters.label: Filament-extruder compatibility
parameters.description: A single 32-bit int encoding the compatibility level of a filament across all extruders (up to 10). Every 3 bits represent one extruder (bits [3*i, 3*i+2] for extruder i). 0: printable, 1: error, 2: critical warning, 3: warning, 4-7: reserved

parameters.label: Filament change
parameters.description: The volume of material to prime extruder on tower.

parameters.label: Hotend change
parameters.description: The volume of material required to prime the extruder for a hotend change on the tower.

parameters.label: Preheat temperature delta
parameters.description: Temperature delta applied during pre-heating before tool change.

parameters.label: Wipe tower cooling
parameters.description: Temperature drop before entering filament tower

parameters.label: Interface layer pre-extrusion distance
parameters.description: Pre-extrusion distance for prime tower interface layer (where different materials meet).

parameters.label: Interface layer pre-extrusion length
parameters.description: Pre-extrusion length for prime tower interface layer (where different materials meet).

parameters.label: Tower ironing area
parameters.description: Ironing area for prime tower interface layer (where different materials meet).

parameters.label: Interface layer purge length
parameters.description: Purge length for prime tower interface layer (where different materials meet).

parameters.label: Interface layer print temperature
parameters.description: Print temperature for prime tower interface layer (where different materials meet). If set to -1, use max recommended nozzle temperature.

parameters.label: Enable tower interface features
parameters.description: When enabled, use dedicated temperature, pre-extrusion and purge settings for prime tower interface layers (where different materials meet), to improve multi-material tool change quality.

parameters.label: Softening temperature
parameters.description: The material softens at this temperature, so when the bed temperature is equal to or greater than it, it's highly recommended to open the front door and/or remove the upper glass to avoid cloggings.

parameters.label: Extruder change
parameters.description: To prevent oozing, the nozzle will perform a reverse travel movement for a certain period after the ramming is complete. The setting define the travel time.

parameters.label: Hotend change
parameters.description: To prevent oozing, the nozzle will perform a reverse travel movement for a certain period after the ramming is complete. The setting define the travel time.

parameters.label: Extruder change
parameters.description: To prevent oozing, the nozzle temperature will be cooled during ramming. Therefore, the ramming time must be greater than the cooldown time. 0 means disabled.

parameters.label: Hotend change
parameters.description: To prevent oozing, the nozzle temperature will be cooled during ramming. Note: only a cooldown command and fan activation are triggered, reaching the target temperature is not guaranteed. 0 means disabled.

parameters.label: Price
parameters.description: Filament price. For statistics only

parameters.label: Vendor
parameters.description: Vendor of filament. For show only

parameters.label: Infill direction
parameters.description: Angle for sparse infill pattern, which controls the start or main direction of line

parameters.label: Sparse infill density
parameters.description: Density of internal sparse infill, 100% means solid throughout

parameters.label: Fill multiline
parameters.description: Using multiple lines for the infill pattern, if supported by infill pattern.

parameters.label: Sparse infill pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern for internal sparse infill

parameters.label: Skin infill pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern for skin

parameters.label: Skeleton infill pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern for skeleton

parameters.label: Top surface
parameters.description: Acceleration of top surface infill. Using a lower value may improve top surface quality

parameters.label: Outer wall
parameters.description: Acceleration of outer wall. Using a lower value can improve quality

parameters.label: Inner wall
parameters.description: Acceleration of inner walls. 0 means using normal printing acceleration

parameters.label: Sparse infill
parameters.description: Acceleration of sparse infill. If the value is expressed as a percentage (e.g. 100%), it will be calculated based on the default acceleration.

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Acceleration of initial layer. Using a lower value can improve build plate adhensive

parameters.label: Enable accel_to_decel
parameters.description: Klipper's max_accel_to_decel will be adjusted automatically

parameters.label: accel_to_decel
parameters.description: Klipper's max_accel_to_decel will be adjusted to this percent of acceleration

parameters.label: Default
parameters.description: Default jerk

parameters.label: Outer wall
parameters.description: Jerk of outer walls

parameters.label: Inner wall
parameters.description: Jerk of inner walls

parameters.label: Infill
parameters.description: Jerk of infill

parameters.label: Top surface
parameters.description: Jerk of top surface

parameters.label: First layer
parameters.description: Jerk of first layer

parameters.label: Travel
parameters.description: Jerk of travel

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Line width of initial layer

parameters.label: Initial layer height
parameters.description: Height of initial layer. Making initial layer height thick slightly can improve build plate adhension

parameters.label: Adaptive layer height
parameters.description: Enabling this option means the height of every layer except the first will be automatically calculated during slicing according to the slope of the model’s surface. Note that this option only takes effect if no prime tower is generated in current plate.

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Speed of initial layer except the solid infill part

parameters.label: Initial layer infill
parameters.description: Speed of solid infill part of initial layer

parameters.label: Initial layer
parameters.description: Nozzle temperature to print initial layer when using this filament

parameters.label: Full fan speed at layer
parameters.description: Fan speed will be ramped up linearly from zero at layer \  to maximum at layer \ . \ will be ignored if lower than \ , in which case the fan will be running at maximum allowed speed at layer \ + 1.

parameters.label: Fuzzy Skin
parameters.description: Randomly jitter while printing the wall, so that the surface has a rough look. This setting controls the fuzzy position

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin thickness
parameters.description: The width within which to jitter. It's adversed to be below outer wall line width

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin point distance
parameters.description: The average distance between the random points introduced on each line segment

parameters.label: Apply fuzzy skin to first layer
parameters.description: Whether to apply fuzzy skin on the first layer.

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin noise type
parameters.description: Noise type to use for fuzzy skin generation: Classic: Classic uniform random noise. Perlin: Perlin noise, which gives a more consistent texture. Billow: Similar to perlin noise, but clumpier. Ridged Multifractal: Ridged noise with sharp, jagged features. Creates marble-like textures. Voronoi: Divides the surface into voronoi cells, and displaces each one by a random amount. Creates a patchwork texture.

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin feature size
parameters.description: The base size of the coherent noise features, in mm. Higher values will result in larger features.

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin noise octaves
parameters.description: The number of octaves of coherent noise to use. Higher values increase the detail of the noise, but also increase computation time.

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin noise persistence
parameters.description: The decay rate for higher octaves of the coherent noise. Lower values will result in smoother noise.

parameters.label: Fuzzy skin generator mode
parameters.description: Displacement: Pattern is formed by shifting the nozzle sideways from the original path. Extrusion: Pattern is formed by varying the amount of extruded plastic (nozzle path stays straight). Combined: Displacement + Extrusion. Similar look to Displacement but fills gaps between perimeters. Note: Extrusion and Combined only work when fuzzy skin thickness is not greater than the printed line width.

parameters.label: Filter out tiny gaps
parameters.description: Filter out gaps smaller than the threshold specified. Gaps smaller than this threshold will be ignored

parameters.label: Precise wall
parameters.description: Improve shell precision by adjusting outer wall spacing. This also improves layer consistency.

parameters.label: Gap infill
parameters.description: Speed of gap infill. Gap usually has irregular line width and should be printed more slowly

parameters.label: Precise Z height
parameters.description: Enable this to get precise z height of object after slicing. It will get the precise object height by fine-tuning the layer heights of the last few layers. Note that this is an experimental parameter.

parameters.label: Arc fitting
parameters.description: Enable this to get a G-code file which has G2 and G3 moves. And the fitting tolerance is the same as resolution

parameters.label: Add line number
parameters.description: Enable this to add line number(Nx) at the beginning of each G-Code line

parameters.label: Scan first layer
parameters.description: Enable this to enable the camera on printer to check the quality of first layer

parameters.label: Print loops in clockwise
parameters.description: Print in clockwise when enabled, or counterclockwise when not enabled, not work for spiral vase mode

parameters.label: Enable clumping detection
parameters.description: Enable clumping detection

parameters.label: Order-independent overlap carving
parameters.description: When enabled, overlapping model parts are carved by bounding-box size so smaller embedded parts are not removed by larger parts due to volume order.

parameters.label: Clumping detection layers
parameters.description: Clumping detection layers.

parameters.label: Probing exclude area of clumping
parameters.description: Probing exclude area of clumping.

parameters.label: Thumbnail size
parameters.description: Decides the size of thumbnail stored in gcode files

parameters.label: Enable spaghetti detector
parameters.description: Enable the camera on printer to check spaghetti

parameters.label: Nozzle type
parameters.description: The metallic material of nozzle. This determines the abrasive resistance of nozzle, and what kind of filament can be printed

parameters.label: Printer structure
parameters.description: The physical arrangement and components of a printing device

parameters.label: Best object position
parameters.description: Best auto arranging position in range [0,1] w.r.t. bed shape.

parameters.label: Auxiliary part cooling fan
parameters.description: Enable this option if machine has auxiliary part cooling fan

parameters.label: Fan direction
parameters.description: Cooling fan direction of the printer

parameters.label: Support control chamber temperature
parameters.description: This option is enabled if machine support controlling chamber temperature

parameters.label: Air filtration enhancement
parameters.description: Enable this if printer support air filtration enhancement.

parameters.label: Use cooling filter
parameters.description: Enable this if printer support cooling filter

parameters.label: G-code flavor
parameters.description: What kind of gcode the printer is compatible with

parameters.label: Exclude objects
parameters.description: Enable this option to add EXCLUDE OBJECT command in g-code for klipper firmware printer

parameters.label: Infill combination
parameters.description: Automatically Combine sparse infill of several layers to print together to reduce time. Wall is still printed with original layer height.

parameters.label: Infill shift step
parameters.description: This parameter adds a slight displacement to each layer of infill to create a cross texture.

parameters.label: Infill rotate step
parameters.description: This parameter adds a slight rotation to each layer of infill to create a cross texture.

parameters.label: Skeleton infill density
parameters.description: The remaining part of the model contour after removing a certain depth from the surface is called the skeleton. This parameter is used to adjust the density of this section. When two regions have the same sparse infill settings but different skeleton densities, their skeleton areas will develop overlapping sections. default is as same as infill density.

parameters.label: Skin infill density
parameters.description: The portion of the model's outer surface within a certain depth range is called the skin. This parameter is used to adjust the density of this section. When two regions have the same sparse infill settings but different skin densities, This area will not be split into two separate regions. default is as same as infill density.

parameters.label: Skin infill depth
parameters.description: The parameter sets the depth of skin.

parameters.label: Infill lock depth
parameters.description: The parameter sets the overlapping depth between the interior and skin.

parameters.label: Skin line width
parameters.description: Adjust the line width of the selected skin paths.

parameters.label: Skeleton line width
parameters.description: Adjust the line width of the selected skeleton paths.

parameters.label: Symmetric infill y axis
parameters.description: If the model has two parts that are symmetric about the y-axis, and you want these parts to have symmetric textures, please click this option on one of the parts.

parameters.label: Lattice angle 1
parameters.description: The angle of the first set of 2D lattice elements in the Z direction. Zero is vertical.

parameters.label: Lattice angle 2
parameters.description: The angle of the second set of 2D lattice elements in the Z direction. Zero is vertical.

parameters.label: Length of sparse infill anchor
parameters.description: Connect a sparse infill line to an internal perimeter with a short segment of an additional perimeter. If expressed as percentage (example: 15%) it is calculated over sparse infill line width. Slicer tries to connect two close infill lines to a short perimeter segment. If no such perimeter segment shorter than infill_anchor_max is found, the infill line is connected to a perimeter segment at just one side and the length of the perimeter segment taken is limited to this parameter, but no longer than anchor_length_max. Set this parameter to zero to disable anchoring perimeters connected to a single infill line.

parameters.label: Maximum length of sparse infill anchor
parameters.description: Connect a sparse infill line to an internal perimeter with a short segment of an additional perimeter. If expressed as percentage (example: 15%) it is calculated over sparse infill line width. Slicer tries to connect two close infill lines to a short perimeter segment. If no such perimeter segment shorter than this parameter is found, the infill line is connected to a perimeter segment at just one side and the length of the perimeter segment taken is limited to infill_anchor, but no longer than this parameter. Set this parameter to zero to disable anchoring.

parameters.label: Sparse infill filament
parameters.description: Filament to print internal sparse infill.

parameters.label: Sparse infill
parameters.label_ja: スパースインフィルライン幅
parameters.description: Line width of internal sparse infill

parameters.label: Infill/Wall overlap
parameters.description: Infill area is enlarged slightly to overlap with wall for better bonding. The percentage value is relative to line width of sparse infill

parameters.label: Sparse infill
parameters.description: Speed of internal sparse infill

parameters.label: Inherits profile
parameters.description: Name of parent profile

parameters.label: Interface shells
parameters.description: Force the generation of solid shells between adjacent materials/volumes. Useful for multi-extruder prints with translucent materials or manual soluble support material

parameters.label: Maximum width of a segmented region
parameters.description: Maximum width of a segmented region. Zero disables this feature.

parameters.label: Interlocking depth of a segmented region
parameters.description: Interlocking depth of a segmented region. It will be ignored if \ is zero or if \  is bigger then \ . Zero disables this feature.

parameters.label: Use beam interlocking
parameters.description: Generate interlocking beam structure at the locations where different filaments touch. This improves the adhesion between filaments, especially models printed in different materials.

parameters.label: Interlocking beam width
parameters.description: The width of the interlocking structure beams.

parameters.label: Interlocking direction
parameters.description: Orientation of interlock beams.

parameters.label: Interlocking beam layers
parameters.description: The height of the beams of the interlocking structure, measured in number of layers. Less layers is stronger, but more prone to defects.

parameters.label: Interlocking depth
parameters.description: The distance from the boundary between filaments to generate interlocking structure, measured in cells. Too few cells will result in poor adhesion.

parameters.label: Interlocking boundary avoidance
parameters.description: The distance from the outside of a model where interlocking structures will not be generated, measured in cells.

parameters.label: Ironing Type
parameters.description: Ironing is using small flow to print on same height of surface again to make flat surface more smooth. This setting controls which layer being ironed

parameters.label: Ironing flow
parameters.description: The amount of material to extrude during ironing. Relative to flow of normal layer height. Too high value results in overextrusion on the surface

parameters.label: Ironing line spacing
parameters.description: The distance between the lines of ironing

parameters.label: Ironing inset
parameters.description: The distance to keep the from the edges of ironing line. 0 means not apply.

parameters.label: Ironing speed
parameters.description: Print speed of ironing lines

parameters.label: ironing direction
parameters.description: Angle for ironing, which controls the relative angle between the top surface and ironing

parameters.label: Layer change G-code
parameters.description: This gcode part is inserted at every layer change after lift z

parameters.label: Supports silent mode
parameters.description: Whether the machine supports silent mode in which machine use lower acceleration to print

parameters.label: Pause G-code
parameters.description: This G-code will be used as a code for the pause print. User can insert pause G-code in gcode viewer

parameters.label: Custom G-code
parameters.description: This G-code will be used as a custom code

parameters.label: Support fast purge mode
parameters.description: Whether this printer supports fast purge mode with optimized temperature and multiplier.

parameters.label: Maximum speed %1%
parameters.description: Maximum speed of %1% axis

parameters.label: Maximum force of the Y axis
parameters.description: The allowed maximum output force of Y axis

parameters.label: Bed mass of the Y axis
parameters.description: The machine bed mass load of Y axis

parameters.label: The allowed max printed mass
parameters.description: The allowed max printed mass on a plate

parameters.label: Minimum speed for extruding
parameters.description: Minimum speed for extruding (M205 S)

parameters.label: Minimum travel speed
parameters.description: Minimum travel speed (M205 T)

parameters.label: Maximum acceleration for extruding
parameters.description: Maximum acceleration for extruding (M204 P)

parameters.label: Maximum acceleration for retracting
parameters.description: Maximum acceleration for retracting (M204 R)

parameters.label: Maximum acceleration for travel
parameters.description: Maximum acceleration for travel (M204 T)

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Part cooling fan speed may be increased when auto cooling is enabled. This is the maximum speed limitation of part cooling fan

parameters.label: Max
parameters.description: The largest printable layer height for extruder. Used to limit the maximum layer height when enable adaptive layer height

parameters.label: Max volumetric slope positive
parameters.description: This experimental setting is used to limit the speed of change in extrusion rate. A value of 1.8 mm³/s² ensures, that a change from the extrusion rate of 1.8 mm³/s (0.45mm extrusion width, 0.2mm extrusion height, feedrate 20 mm/s) to 5.4 mm³/s (feedrate 60 mm/s) will take at least 2 seconds.

parameters.label: Max volumetric slope negative
parameters.description: This experimental setting is used to limit the speed of change in extrusion rate. A value of 1.8 mm³/s² ensures, that a change from the extrusion rate of 1.8 mm³/s (0.45mm extrusion width, 0.2mm extrusion height, feedrate 20 mm/s) to 5.4 mm³/s (feedrate 60 mm/s) will take at least 2 seconds.

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Minimum speed for part cooling fan

parameters.label: Fan speed
parameters.description: Speed of auxiliary part cooling fan. Auxiliary fan will run at this speed during printing except the first several layers which are defined by no cooling layers

parameters.label: Min
parameters.description: The lowest printable layer height for extruder. Used to limit the minimum layer height when enable adaptive layer height

parameters.label: Min print speed
parameters.description: The minimum printing speed when slow down for cooling

parameters.label: Nozzle diameter
parameters.description: Diameter of nozzle

parameters.label: Host Type
parameters.description: Slic3r can upload G-code files to a printer host. This field must contain the kind of the host.

parameters.label: Nozzle volume
parameters.description: Volume of nozzle between the cutter and the end of nozzle

parameters.label: Start end points
parameters.description: The start and end points which are from cutter area to garbage can.

parameters.label: Reduce infill retraction
parameters.description: Controls whether retraction is skipped when traveling within the infill area. \ enables this optimization for filaments with low metal stickiness (e.g. PLA) and disables it for medium/high metal stickiness filaments (e.g. PETG) to avoid oozing artifacts. \ always skips retraction in infill areas regardless of filament type. \ always retracts normally.

parameters.label: Enable
parameters.description: This option will drop the temperature of the inactive extruders to prevent oozing. It will enable a tall skirt automatically and move extruders outside such skirt when changing temperatures.

parameters.label: Filename format
parameters.description: User can self-define the project file name when export

parameters.label: Detect overhang wall
parameters.description: Detect the overhang percentage relative to line width and use different speed to print. For 100 percent overhang, bridge speed is used.

parameters.label: Smooth speed discontinuity area
parameters.description: Add the speed transition between discontinuity area.

parameters.label: Smooth coefficient
parameters.description: The smaller the number, the longer the speed transition path. 0 means not apply.

parameters.label: Walls
parameters.description: Filament to print walls

parameters.label: Inner wall
parameters.description: Line width of inner wall

parameters.label: Inner wall
parameters.description: Speed of inner wall

parameters.label: Wall loops
parameters.description: Number of walls of every layer

parameters.label: Embedding the wall into the infill
parameters.description: Embedding the wall into parts where the wall loops are absent ensures that the wall connects seamlessly to the infill.

parameters.label: Alternate extra wall
parameters.description: Add an extra wall on alternating layers to improve layer bonding and part strength without the full cost of a permanent extra wall.

parameters.label: Post-processing Scripts
parameters.description: If you want to process the output G-code through custom scripts, just list their absolute paths here. Separate multiple scripts with a semicolon. Scripts will be passed the absolute path to the G-code file as the first argument, and variables of settings also can be read

parameters.label: Printer type
parameters.description: Type of the printer

parameters.label: Printer variant
parameters.description: Name of the printer variant. For example, the printer variants may be differentiated by a nozzle diameter.

parameters.label: Raft contact Z distance
parameters.description: Z gap between object and raft. Ignored for soluble interface

parameters.label: Raft expansion
parameters.description: Expand all raft layers in XY plane

parameters.label: Initial layer density
parameters.description: Density of the first raft or support layer

parameters.label: Initial layer expansion
parameters.description: Expand the first raft or support layer to improve bed plate adhesion, -1 means auto

parameters.label: Raft layers
parameters.description: Object will be raised by this number of support layers. Use this function to avoid warping when print ABS

parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description: G-code path is generated after simplifying the contour of model to avoid too many points and gcode lines in the gcode file. Smaller value means higher resolution and more time to slice

parameters.label: Travel distance threshold
parameters.description: Only trigger retraction when the travel distance is longer than this threshold

parameters.label: Retract amount before wipe
parameters.description: The length of fast retraction before wipe, relative to retraction length

parameters.label: Retract when change layer
parameters.description: Force a retraction when changes layer

parameters.label: Length
parameters.description: Some amount of material in extruder is pulled back to avoid ooze during long travel. Set zero to disable retraction

parameters.label: Long retraction when cut(experimental)
parameters.description: Experimental feature.Retracting and cutting off the filament at a longer distance during changes to minimize purge. While this reduces flush significantly, it may also raise the risk of nozzle clogs or other printing problems.

parameters.label: Retraction distance when cut
parameters.description: Experimental feature.Retraction length before cutting off during filament change

parameters.label: Length
parameters.description: When retraction is triggered before changing tool, filament is pulled back by the specified amount (the length is measured on raw filament, before it enters the extruder).

parameters.label: Z hop when retract
parameters.description: Whenever the retraction is done, the nozzle is lifted a little to create clearance between nozzle and the print. It prevents nozzle from hitting the print when travel moves. Using spiral line to lift z can prevent stringing

parameters.label: Z hop lower boundary
parameters.description: Z hop will only come into effect when Z is above this value and is below the parameter: \

parameters.label: Z hop upper boundary
parameters.description: If this value is positive, Z hop will only come into effect when Z is above the parameter: \ and is below this value

parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: This setting is only used for initial value of manual calibration of pressure advance. Bowden extruder usually has larger pa value. This setting doesn't influence normal slicing

parameters.label: Nozzle Volume Type
parameters.description: Nozzle volume type

parameters.label: Default Nozzle Volume Type
parameters.description: Default Nozzle volume type for extruders in this printer

parameters.label: Extruder variant list
parameters.description: Extruder variant list

parameters.label: Extruder ams count
parameters.description: Ams counts of per extruder

parameters.label: Enable filament dynamic map
parameters.description: Support filament map to different nozzle

parameters.label: Has filament switcher
parameters.description: Whether a filament switcher is connected to the printer

parameters.label: extruder nozzle count
parameters.description: extruder nozzle count

parameters.label: extruder nozzle volume type
parameters.description: extruder nozzle volume type

parameters.label: Printer extruder id
parameters.description: Printer extruder id

parameters.label: Printer's extruder variant
parameters.description: Printer's extruder variant

parameters.label: Master extruder id
parameters.description: Default extruder id to place filament

parameters.label: Print extruder id
parameters.description: Print extruder id

parameters.label: Print's extruder variant
parameters.description: Print's extruder variant

parameters.label: Filament extruder id
parameters.description: Filament extruder id

parameters.label: Filament's extruder variant
parameters.description: Filament's extruder variant

parameters.label: Filament self index
parameters.description: Filament self index

parameters.label: length when change hotend
parameters.description: When this retraction value is modified, it will be used as the amount of filament retracted inside the hotend before changing hotends.

parameters.label: Extra length on restart
parameters.description: When the retraction is compensated after the travel move, the extruder will push this additional amount of filament. This setting is rarely needed.

parameters.label: Extra length on restart
parameters.description: When the retraction is compensated after changing tool, the extruder will push this additional amount of filament.

parameters.label: Retraction Speed
parameters.description: Speed of retractions

parameters.label: Deretraction Speed
parameters.description: Speed for reloading filament into extruder. Zero means the same speed as retraction

parameters.label: Seam position
parameters.description: The start position to print each part of outer wall

parameters.label: Seam placement away from overhangs(experimental)
parameters.description: Ensure seam placement away from overhangs for alignment and backing modes.

parameters.label: Seam gap
parameters.description: In order to reduce the visibility of the seam in a closed loop extrusion, the loop is interrupted and shortened by a specified amount. This amount as a percentage of the current extruder diameter. The default value for this parameter is 15

parameters.label: Smart scarf seam application
parameters.description: Apply scarf joints only to smooth perimeters where traditional seams do not conceal the seams at sharp corners effectively.

parameters.label: Scarf application angle threshold
parameters.description: This option sets the threshold angle for applying a conditional scarf joint seam. If the seam angle within the perimeter loop exceeds this value (indicating the absence of sharp corners), a scarf joint seam will be used. The default value is 155°.

parameters.label: Scarf around entire wall
parameters.description: The scarf extends to the entire length of the wall.

parameters.label: Scarf steps
parameters.description: Minimum number of segments of each scarf.

parameters.label: Scarf joint for inner walls
parameters.description: Use scarf joint for inner walls as well.

parameters.label: Override filament scarf seam setting
parameters.description: Overrider filament scarf seam setting and could control settings by modifier.

parameters.label: Scarf seam type
parameters.description: Set scarf seam type for this filament. This setting could minimize seam visibiliy.

parameters.label: Scarf start height
parameters.description: This amount can be specified in millimeters or as a percentage of the current layer height.

parameters.label: Scarf slope gap
parameters.description: In order to reduce the visiblity of the seam in closed loop, the inner wall and outer wall are shortened by a specified amount.

parameters.label: Scarf length
parameters.description: Length of the scarf. Setting this parameter to zero effectively disables the scarf.

parameters.label: Wipe speed
parameters.description: The wipe speed is determined by the speed setting specified in this configuration. If the value is expressed as a percentage (e.g. 80%), it will be calculated based on the travel speed setting above. The default value for this parameter is 80%

parameters.label: Role-based wipe speed
parameters.description: The wipe speed is determined by speed of current extrusion role. e.g if a wipe action is executed immediately following an outer wall extrusion, the speed of the outer wall extrusion will be utilized for the wipe action.

parameters.label: Skirt distance
parameters.description: Distance from skirt to brim or object

parameters.label: Skirt per object
parameters.description: Generate independent skirt around each object in sequential printing mode. When disabled, a single skirt is drawn around all objects.

parameters.label: Skirt height
parameters.description: How many layers of skirt. Usually only one layer

parameters.label: Draft shield
parameters.description: With draft shield active, the skirt will be printed skirt_distance from the object, possibly intersecting brim. Enabled = skirt is as tall as the highest printed object. Limited = skirt is as tall as specified by skirt_height. This is useful to protect an ABS or ASA print from warping and detaching from print bed due to wind draft.

parameters.label: Skirt loops
parameters.description: Number of loops for the skirt. Zero means disabling skirt

parameters.label: Layer time
parameters.description: The printing speed in exported gcode will be slowed down, when the estimated layer time is shorter than this value, to get better cooling for these layers

parameters.label: Minimum sparse infill threshold
parameters.description: Sparse infill area which is smaller than threshold value is replaced by internal solid infill

parameters.label: Solid infill
parameters.description: Filament to print solid infill

parameters.label: Internal solid infill
parameters.description: Line width of internal solid infill

parameters.label: Internal solid infill
parameters.description: Speed of internal solid infill, not the top and bottom surface

parameters.label: Spiral vase
parameters.description: Spiralize smooths out the z moves of the outer contour. And turns a solid model into a single walled print with solid bottom layers. The final generated model has no seam

parameters.label: Smooth Spiral
parameters.description: Smooth Spiral smoothes out X and Y moves as well resulting in no visible seam at all, even in the XY directions on walls that are not vertical

parameters.label: Max XY Smoothing
parameters.description: Maximum distance to move points in XY to try to achieve a smooth spiral If expressed as a %, it will be computed over nozzle diameter

parameters.label: Timelapse
parameters.description: If smooth or traditional mode is selected, a timelapse video will be generated for each print. After each layer is printed, a snapshot is taken with the chamber camera. All of these snapshots are composed into a timelapse video when printing completes. If smooth mode is selected, the toolhead will move to the excess chute after each layer is printed and then take a snapshot. Since the melt filament may leak from the nozzle during the process of taking a snapshot, prime tower is required for smooth mode to wipe nozzle.

parameters.label: Farthest point timelapse
parameters.description: When enabled, the timelapse snapshot is taken at the farthest point from camera instead of traveling to the wipe tower or excess chute. Only effective in traditional timelapse mode on non-I3 printers.

parameters.label: Temperature variation
parameters.description: Temperature difference to be applied when an extruder is not active. Enables a full-height \ skirt on which the nozzles are periodically wiped.

parameters.label: Start G-code
parameters.description: Start G-code when start the whole printing
parameters.default: G28 ; home all axes G1 Z5 F5000 ; lift nozzle

parameters.label: Start G-code
parameters.description: Start G-code when start the printing of this filament

parameters.label: Single Extruder Multi Material
parameters.description: Use single nozzle to print multi filament

parameters.label: No sparse layers (EXPERIMENTAL)
parameters.description: If enabled, the wipe tower will not be printed on layers with no toolchanges. On layers with a toolchange, extruder will travel downward to print the wipe tower. User is responsible for ensuring there is no collision with the print.

parameters.label: Slice gap closing radius
parameters.description: Cracks smaller than 2x gap closing radius are being filled during the triangle mesh slicing. The gap closing operation may reduce the final print resolution, therefore it is advisable to keep the value reasonably low.

parameters.label: Slicing Mode
parameters.description: Use \ for 3DLabPrint airplane models. Use \ to close all holes in the model.

parameters.label: Enable support
parameters.description: Enable support generation.

parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: normal(auto) and tree(auto) is used to generate support automatically. If normal(manual) or tree(manual) is selected, only support enforcers are generated

parameters.label: Support/object xy distance
parameters.description: XY separation between an object and its support

parameters.label: Support/object first layer gap
parameters.description: XY separation between an object and its support at the first layer.

parameters.label: Pattern angle
parameters.description: Use this setting to rotate the support pattern on the horizontal plane.

parameters.label: On build plate only
parameters.description: Don't create support on model surface, only on build plate

parameters.label: Support critical regions only
parameters.description: Only create support for critical regions including sharp tail, cantilever, etc.

parameters.label: Remove small overhangs
parameters.description: Remove small overhangs that possibly need no supports.

parameters.label: Top Z distance
parameters.description: The z gap between the top support interface and object

parameters.label: Bottom Z distance
parameters.description: The z gap between the bottom support interface and object

parameters.label: Enforce support for the first
parameters.description: Generate support material for the specified number of layers counting from bottom, regardless of whether normal support material is enabled or not and regardless of any angle threshold. This is useful for getting more adhesion of objects having a very thin or poor footprint on the build plate.

parameters.label: Support/raft base
parameters.description: Filament to print support base and raft. \ means no specific filament for support and current filament is used

parameters.label: Avoid interface filament for base
parameters.description: Avoid using support interface filament to print support base if possible.

parameters.label: Support
parameters.description: Line width of support

parameters.label: Interface use loop pattern
parameters.description: Cover the top contact layer of the supports with loops. Disabled by default.

parameters.label: Support/raft interface
parameters.description: Filament to print support interface. \ means no specific filament for support interface and current filament is used

parameters.label: Top interface layers
parameters.description: Number of top interface layers

parameters.label: Bottom interface layers
parameters.description: Number of bottom interface layers

parameters.label: Top interface spacing
parameters.description: Spacing of interface lines. Zero means solid interface. And zero spacing is required to access the enable_support_ironing option

parameters.label: Enable ironing support interface
parameters.description: Ironing is using small flow to print on same height of surface again to make flat surface more smooth. This setting controls whether support interface or raft interface will be ironned.Support ironing could only works on solid interface, that is, support_interface_spacing was zero and support_interface_pattern was't grid

parameters.label: Support ironing flow
parameters.description: The amount of material to extrude during ironing. Relative to flow of normal layer height. Too high value results in overextrusion on the surface

parameters.label: Support ironing line spacing
parameters.description: The distance between the lines of ironing

parameters.label: Support ironing inset
parameters.description: The distance to keep the from the edges of ironing line. 0 means not apply.

parameters.label: Support ironing speed
parameters.description: Print speed of ironing lines

parameters.label: Support ironing direction
parameters.description: Angle for ironing, which controls the relative angle between the top surface and ironing

parameters.label: Bottom interface spacing
parameters.description: Spacing of bottom interface lines. Zero means solid interface

parameters.label: Support interface
parameters.description: Speed of support interface

parameters.label: Base pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern of support

parameters.label: Interface pattern
parameters.description: Line pattern of support interface. Default pattern for support interface is Rectilinear Interlaced

parameters.label: Base pattern spacing
parameters.description: Spacing between support lines

parameters.label: Normal Support expansion
parameters.description: Expand (+) or shrink (-) the horizontal span of normal support

parameters.label: Support
parameters.description: Speed of support

parameters.label: Style
parameters.description: Style and shape of the support. For normal support, projecting the supports into a regular grid will create more stable supports (default), while snug support towers will save material and reduce object scarring. For tree support, slim style will merge branches more aggressively and save a lot of material, strong style will make larger and stronger support structure and use more materials, while hybrid style is the combination of slim tree and normal support with normal nodes under large flat overhangs. Organic style will produce more organic shaped tree structure and less interfaces which makes it easer to be removed. The default style is organic tree for most cases, and hybrid tree if adaptive layer height or soluble interface is enabled.

parameters.label: Z overrides X/Y
parameters.description: When top z distance overrides support/object xy distance, give priority to ensuring that supports are generated beneath overhangs, and a gap of the same size as top z distance is leaved with the model. Whereas in the opposite case, the gap between supports and the model follows support/object xy distance all the time. Only recommended to enable when using HybridTree.

parameters.label: Independent support layer height
parameters.description: Support layer uses layer height independent with object layer. This is to support customizing z-gap and save print time. This option will be invalid when the prime tower is enabled.

parameters.label: Threshold angle
parameters.description: Support will be generated for overhangs whose slope angle is below the threshold.

parameters.label: Branch angle
parameters.description: This setting determines the maximum overhang angle that t he branches of tree support allowed to make. If the angle is increased, the branches can be printed more horizontally, allowing them to reach farther.

parameters.label: Branch distance
parameters.description: This setting determines the distance between neighboring tree support nodes.

parameters.label: Branch diameter
parameters.description: This setting determines the initial diameter of support nodes.

parameters.label: Branch diameter angle
parameters.description: The angle of the branches' diameter as they gradually become thicker towards the bottom. An angle of 0 will cause the branches to have uniform thickness over their length. A bit of an angle can increase stability of the tree support.

parameters.label: Support wall loops
parameters.description: This setting specifies the count of support walls in the range of [-1,2]. -1 means auto, and 0 means allowing infill-only mode where support is thick enough.

parameters.label: Chamber temperature
parameters.description: Higher chamber temperature can help suppress or reduce warping and potentially lead to higher interlayer bonding strength for high temperature materials like ABS, ASA, PC, PA and so on. At the same time, the air filtration of ABS and ASA will get worse.While for PLA, PETG, TPU, PVA and other low temperature materials, the actual chamber temperature should not be high to avoid cloggings, so 0 which stands for turning off is highly recommended

parameters.label: Other layers
parameters.description: Nozzle temperature for layers after the initial one

parameters.label: Detect thin wall
parameters.description: Detect thin wall which can't contain two line width. And use single line to print. Maybe printed not very well, because it's not closed loop

parameters.label: Change filament G-code
parameters.description: This gcode is inserted when change filament, including T command to trigger tool change

parameters.label: Top surface
parameters.description: Line width for top surfaces

parameters.label: Top surface
parameters.description: Speed of top surface infill which is solid

parameters.label: Top shell layers
parameters.description: This is the number of solid layers of top shell, including the top surface layer. When the thickness calculated by this value is thinner than top shell thickness, the top shell layers will be increased

parameters.label: Top shell thickness
parameters.description: The number of top solid layers is increased when slicing if the thickness calculated by top shell layers is thinner than this value. This can avoid having too thin shell when layer height is small. 0 means that this setting is disabled and thickness of top shell is absolutely determained by top shell layers

parameters.label: Top paint penetration layers
parameters.description: This is  the number of layers of top paint penetration.

parameters.label: Bottom paint penetration layers
parameters.description: This is the number of layers of bottom paint penetration.

parameters.label: Use infill instead of top and bottom surfaces
parameters.description: Using infill instead of top and bottom surfaces.

parameters.label: Travel
parameters.description: Speed of travel which is faster and without extrusion

parameters.label: Z travel
parameters.description: Speed of vertical travel along z axis. This is typically lower because build plate or gantry is hard to be moved. Zero means using travel speed directly in gcode, but will be limited by printer's ability when run gcode

parameters.label: Use relative E distances
parameters.description: If your firmware requires relative E values, check this, otherwise leave it unchecked. Must use relative e distance for Bambu printer

parameters.label: Use firmware retraction
parameters.description: Convert the retraction moves to G10 and G11 gcode

parameters.label: Wipe while retracting
parameters.description: Move nozzle along the last extrusion path when retracting to clean leaked material on nozzle. This can minimize blob when printing new part after travel

parameters.label: Wipe Distance
parameters.description: Describe how long the nozzle will move along the last path when retracting

parameters.label: Mixed color sublayer
parameters.description: Enable mixed color sublayer splitting. When enabled, layers containing mixed color filaments will be split into sub-layers to achieve color mixing effects.

parameters.label: Enable
parameters.description: The wiping tower can be used to clean up the residue on the nozzle and stabilize the chamber pressure inside the nozzle, in order to avoid appearance defects when printing objects.

parameters.label: Internal ribs
parameters.description: Enable internal ribs to increase the stability of the prime tower.

parameters.label: Auto circle contour-hole compensation
parameters.description: Expirment feature to compensate the circle holes and circle contour. This feature is used to improve the accuracy of the circle holes and contour within the diameter below 50mm. Only support PLA Basic, PLA CF, PET CF, PETG CF and PETG HF.

parameters.label: User Customized Offset
parameters.description: If you want to have tighter or looser assemble, you can set this value. When it is positive, it indicates tightening, otherwise, it indicates loosening

parameters.label: Scarf Seam On Compensation Circles
parameters.description: Scarf seam will be applied on circles for better dimensional accuracy.

parameters.label: Circle Compensation Speed
parameters.description: circle_compensation_speed

parameters.label: Counter Coef 1
parameters.description: counter_coef_1
